mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPI inflation is not re-accelerating, because rate cuts without renewed inflation pressure are interpreted as policy support rather than recession panic.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is falling over a 3-month window while CPIAUCSL inflation is flat or declining, reject the hypothesis. Also reject if the conditional Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment indicates weakening household income and demand, which pressures corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal becomes actionable only after the unemployment rate crosses above a local threshold, because small labor-market noise does not reliably change equity pricing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.3% and increases by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with rising UNRATE still deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional baseline by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in retail sales signals stronger nominal demand and better near-term revenue growth for large-cap equities, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect should be strongest when sales growth is broad-based rather than a one-month spike, because persistent consumer demand is what feeds earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline after positive 3-month RSAFS growth, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates improving real activity and stronger cyclically sensitive earnings, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is most informative when production growth is positive after prior weakness, because a turn in supply-side activity reflects broadening economic momentum rather than merely stable output.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after a prior negative 3-month change
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO turnarounds do not produce above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal is negative in more than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts downturn transmits to equities through weaker construction activity, lower durable-goods demand, and softer cyclical expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should activate only when starts fall through a recent moving-average floor, because mild monthly noise does not alter earnings forecasts.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 8%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST breakdowns are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the average forward return is not materially below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated while the policy rate is still restrictive, real rates stay high and equity multiples compress, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal requires both a high CPI level and a policy rate above a restrictive threshold, because inflation alone does not imply tighter financial conditions unless policy remains tight too.
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-policy-rate regimes do not underperform over 20 trading days, or if SPX forward returns are not lower than baseline when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 12-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth strength supports risk assets by confirming income growth and lowering recession probability, so positive PAYEMS momentum should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is fastest at short horizons because labor-market surprises are quickly repriced into growth expectations and near-term sector leadership.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by at least 0.15% month over month for 2 consecutive releases
failure_condition: Reject if payroll-strength triggers fail to produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the short-horizon Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising GDP and rising retail sales signals synchronized final demand, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The joint condition matters because either series alone can be noisy, but persistent co-movement confirms economy-wide demand acceleration.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous positive GDPC1 and RSAFS growth does not outperform baseline over 60 trading days, or if the joint signal is not positive in at least 55% of qualifying observations.
